message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Luca respawned, code reset, converge on tasks">
Team status update:

**Luca has been shut down and respawned.** All of his uncommitted work (scaffolding, registration code, templates, migrations) has been removed. The working tree is clean.

**Current state:**
- 3 local commits (not pushed): Marty's docs, Lea's CSS tokens, updated user stories
- No Rust code exists yet — no Cargo.toml, no src/, no tests/
- Tasks 1 and 2 are reset to pending
- The new Luca is starting fresh with Task 1 (scaffolding), with strict commit-after-every-step discipline

**Important: Scott and others** — your analysis of the "current codebase" and registration code is no longer valid. That code has been removed. We're starting clean from the scaffolding step.

**The task breakdown discussion is still open.** I've heard from Kent, Marty, Heydon, Scott, Steve Schoger, Steve Krug, and Lea. Kent proposed adding "base template + static serving" and "session infrastructure" as new tasks. Scott recommends just adding session infrastructure. Please converge on a final consensus so I can update the task list.

**Do NOT start any feature work until the task breakdown is agreed upon.** Luca is only doing scaffolding (Tasks 1 + 2). Everything else waits for consensus.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Understood. The codebase has been reset -- all the Rust code I reviewed earlier (domain types, registration service, etc.) is gone. My accessibility requirements for that code are still valid as design guidance, but I need to be aware that none of it exists yet.

Let me focus on the task breakdown consensus. Kent proposed adding "base template + static serving" and "session infrastructure." Scott recommends just session infrastructure. I proposed the base template task. Let me converge on a position and communicate it clearly.

content.text: Good -- the CSS update is staged. Tasks 1 and 2 are back to pending. Luca is being respawned. Let me converge on the task breakdown and communicate my position clearly to the team lead.

input.content:
Team lead, here's my converged position on the task breakdown. I'll keep it concrete.

**I support Kent's proposal to add both "Base Template + Static Serving" and "Session Infrastructure" as separate tasks.**

Here's my reasoning:

**Base Template + Static Serving (should go between Task #2 and Registration):**
This is not optional from an accessibility perspective. Every page needs:
- `<html lang="en">`
- Skip link to main content (WCAG 2.4.1 Level A -- literally the baseline)
- Landmark regions (`<main>`, `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<footer>`)
- Dynamic `<title>` element
- Viewport meta without zoom restrictions
- CSS link (Lea's foundation)
- HTMX script tag
- Status announcer (`aria-live="polite"` region for screen reader announcements)

If we don't build this as a separate task, it will be part of the registration task, making that task much larger and mixing infrastructure with feature work. The base template is independently testable -- we can assert on landmarks, skip link, heading hierarchy, and lang attribute before any feature exists.

**Session Infrastructure (should go between Base Template and Registration):**
I defer to Scott and Luca on whether this is truly separable. From my perspective, it doesn't affect accessibility directly -- it's plumbing. But if Scott says it's a cleaner separation, I trust his architectural judgment.

**My final proposed task order:**
1. Project scaffolding (Luca driving now)
2. Walking skeleton / health check
3. ~~Design tokens~~ (done)
4. **Base HTML template + static file serving** (NEW)
5. **Session infrastructure** (NEW -- if Scott/Luca agree it's separable)
6. Registration (US-1)
7. Login + Logout (US-2 + US-3)
8. View list + Add todo (US-4 + US-5)
9. Complete todo (US-6)
10. Delete todo (US-7)
11. Edit todo title (US-8)

I believe this aligns with Kent, Scott, and Marty. I haven't heard objections from Carson, Lea, Steve S, or Steve K on this structure. If the team is ready to converge, I'm a "consent" -- I actively support this breakdown.
input.summary: Converged task breakdown position - support both new tasks

content.text: Now let me also note the important context change for my earlier work -- the registration code I reviewed is gone, but all my accessibility requirements and design patterns remain valid as guidance for when the code is rebuilt. Let me also check on Lea's CSS update since it's staged.

input.content:
Lea, I see your CSS update is staged but not yet committed. Per the project owner's directive: commit early and often. If you've finished the updates, please commit and push. Also, I noticed in my review that `--color-fg-muted` is still pointing to gray-500 (line 212) rather than gray-600 as you mentioned. And the skip link focus reveal (`.skip-link` class) is still missing.

Can you make those two fixes, then commit everything? We need the CSS foundation locked down before the base template task begins -- whoever builds the template will need the `.skip-link` class for the skip link element.
input.summary: CSS staged but not committed, two fixes still needed

routing.summary: CSS staged but not committed, two fixes still needed

Lea, I see your CSS update is staged but not yet committed. Per the project owner's directive: commit early and often. If you've finished the updates, please commit and push. Also, I noticed in my review that `--color-fg-muted` is still pointing to gray-500 (line 212) rather than gray-600 as you mentioned. And the skip link focus reveal (`.skip-link` class) is still missing.

Can you make those two fixes, then commit everything? We need the CSS foundation locked down before the base template task begins -- whoever builds the template will need the `.skip-link` class for the skip link element.

content.text: I'm now in the right state: aware that the codebase has been reset, my task breakdown position is communicated, and I've nudged Lea on the two remaining CSS fixes. My earlier design guidance (HTMX focus management protocol, form error patterns, todo item interaction patterns, contrast audit) all remains valid and will be applied when the code is rebuilt. Nothing was lost -- the accessibility requirements exist in the team's shared understanding, ready to be applied to whatever Luca builds next.
